questions.question: Which Notion feature allows a page to appear at the top of your sidebar for quick access?

options.text: Pinning
options.explanation: Notion uses "Favorites" for this purpose, not a Pin feature.

options.text: Favorite
options.explanation: Marking a page as a Favorite places it at the top of the sidebar for easy access.

options.text: Bookmark
options.explanation: Bookmarks are used within pages, not for sidebar ordering.

options.text: Shortcut
options.explanation: Shortcuts can be created, but they don't affect sidebar placement.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of centralizing team documents in a single Notion database?

options.text: It reduces the need for version control software.
options.explanation: While centralization helps with versioning, the main benefit highlighted is transparency, accessibility, and consistency.

options.text: It ensures all documents are automatically published to the web.
options.explanation: Publishing is optional; centralization is about internal organization.

options.text: It provides transparency, accessibility, and consistency across the company.
options.explanation: Centralizing docs creates a single source of truth, making information transparent, easy to access, and consistent.

options.text: It eliminates the need for meetings.
options.explanation: Centralization supports meeting efficiency but does not replace meetings altogether.

questions.question: How do "Synced copies" differ from "Synced blocks" in Notion?

options.text: Synced copies duplicate an entire database across pages, while Synced blocks duplicate specific content blocks.
options.explanation: Synced copies replicate a whole database for convenience; Synced blocks keep the same content block in sync across multiple pages.

options.text: Synced copies are only available for text, whereas Synced blocks work with any type of content.
options.explanation: Both can handle various content types; the distinction is scope, not content type.

options.text: Synced copies automatically update external apps, while Synced blocks do not.
options.explanation: Neither feature directly syncs with external apps; they operate within Notion.

options.text: Synced copies are permanent, while Synced blocks can be broken at any time.
options.explanation: Both can be edited or unlinked; permanence isn’t the key difference.

questions.question: When an old document is no longer needed, what does the guide recommend you do instead of deleting it?

options.text: Move it to a history or archive section.
options.explanation: Creating a history or archive preserves information for future reference while keeping the workspace tidy.

options.text: Export it as a PDF and store it on your local drive.
options.explanation: Exporting is possible but the guide specifically suggests an internal archive section.

options.text: Leave it in the original database but mark it as "obsolete".
options.explanation: Marking as obsolete isn’t mentioned; archiving provides a clearer separation.

options.text: Convert it into a template for future use.
options.explanation: Templates are for reusable structures, not for preserving outdated content.

questions.question: Which Notion tool can you use to quickly locate information across your workspace?

options.text: Quick Find
options.explanation: Quick Find is designed to speed up searching within the workspace.

options.text: Page Index
options.explanation: There is no "Page Index" feature mentioned in the guide.

options.text: Sidebar Filter
options.explanation: Sidebar filtering helps view pages but isn’t the primary quick-search tool.

options.text: Document Tracker
options.explanation: Document Tracker is not a Notion feature referenced here.

questions.question: What additional benefit does using the Q&A feature provide beyond Quick Find?

options.text: It automatically creates new documentation based on your questions.
options.explanation: Q&A provides instant answers, not auto-generated docs.

options.text: It offers instant answers to specific questions, reducing the need to search manually.
options.explanation: Q&A delivers immediate answers, complementing the broader search capability of Quick Find.

options.text: It syncs your questions across all team members in real time.
options.explanation: Real‑time syncing isn’t the highlighted advantage of Q&A.

options.text: It archives old questions for future reference.
options.explanation: Archiving is a separate practice; Q&A focuses on answering, not archiving.

questions.question: Why might a startup choose to link related Notion pages together?

options.text: To create a visual map that automatically generates a company org chart.
options.explanation: Linking pages creates connections, but it doesn’t auto‑generate org charts.

options.text: To ensure that updates in one document are reflected in all related documents without duplication.
options.explanation: Linking establishes relationships, making it easier to keep related information consistent without copying content.

options.text: To increase the total number of pages, which improves Notion’s search ranking.
options.explanation: Page count doesn’t affect search ranking; the goal is functional connectivity.

options.text: To satisfy compliance requirements that mandate cross‑referencing every document.
options.explanation: While cross‑referencing can aid compliance, the guide emphasizes practical workflow benefits, not regulatory mandates.

questions.question: According to the guide, where can you find over 10,000 templates to help you get started?

options.text: The Notion Template Gallery
options.explanation: The guide points users to browse over 10,000 templates in the Template Gallery.

options.text: The Notion Community Forum
options.explanation: The forum is a place for discussion, not the primary source of the large template collection.

options.text: The YouTube channel’s video description
options.explanation: YouTube may reference templates, but the guide explicitly mentions the Template Gallery.

options.text: The Notion Help Center article itself
options.explanation: The article links to the gallery; it isn’t the repository of the templates.
